introHeading: Hi, I'm Muthukrishnan — a senior frontend developer who believes the web should feel alive.
bio: I've spent the last 5+ years crafting interfaces that merge thoughtful design with engineering precision. My work lives at the intersection of performance and aesthetics — where sub-second load times meet fluid 60fps animations.

I specialize in the Vue ecosystem — Vue 3, Nuxt 3, and TypeScript are my daily tools. But what truly excites me is pushing the boundaries of what's possible in the browser: WebGL visualizations, complex GSAP timelines, and design systems that scale from a single component to an entire product.

When I'm not writing code, you'll find me exploring new interaction patterns, contributing to open-source, or deep-diving into browser rendering internals. I believe great frontend development is equal parts engineering discipline and creative intuition.

journey.description: Fell in love with component-driven architecture and reactive state management. Built production SPAs from day one.
Led migration of legacy jQuery codebases to Vue 3 with TypeScript. Introduced design systems and automated testing pipelines.
Architected micro-frontend solutions, mentored junior developers, and drove adoption of SSR with Nuxt 3 for performance-critical projects.
Specializing in WebGL experiences, advanced animation systems, and building tools that bridge design and engineering.

philosophy.title: Craft over code
philosophy.text: Every pixel, every transition, every interaction is a chance to create something that feels inevitable — not just functional.

philosophy.title: Performance is UX
philosophy.text: A beautiful interface that loads slowly is a broken interface. I obsess over bundle size, paint timing, and perceived speed.

philosophy.title: Simplicity scales
philosophy.text: The best architecture is the one you can explain in five minutes. I favour composition over configuration, and clarity over cleverness.

philosophy.title: Empathy-driven
philosophy.text: Code is for humans first, machines second. I write for the developer who maintains it at 2 AM and the user who just wants it to work.
